workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: I need you to process this email thread from Lucerne Publishing. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-02-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:06:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:28:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-F trap (Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-F failure mode (Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-F -- Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached
